title: Aleuritia rupicola
intro: Aleuritia rupicola, also known as Rupicola, is a type of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Aleuritia rupicola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aleuritia rupicola is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
